name: Potato Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 meat mixture (ground beef, onion, and egg)
1 sauce (bacon and onions sautéed in bacon fat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the potatoes into a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer. Squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
2. Mix the grated potato with the egg and flour until well combined. Some starch will settle at the bottom; mix it in if desired.
3. Form the mixture into 8 rounded balls.
4. Prepare the meat mixture by combining ground beef, chopped onion, and egg. Form into 8 small balls.
5. Flatten each potato ball and place a meat ball in the center. Roll up the dough around the meat to form zeppelin-shaped pancakes.
6. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Place the pancakes in the boiling water for 30 minutes.
7. Drain the cooked zeppelins on paper towels.
8. Sauté the bacon until crispy, then drain and chop with sautéed onions.
9. Combine the bacon pieces and chopped onions with a splash of sour cream.
10. Serve the warm zeppelin pancakes topped with the bacon-onion sauce.
Note: Some cooks omit flour from the potato mixture, using farina or cream of wheat instead. A slight variation is to cook the meat mixture separately before assembling the pancakes.
